response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software engineer with a solid background in web, distributed systems, and developer tooling, but his profile has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, deep neural network workload optimization, and GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His exposure to AI is limited to MCP tooling and agentic workflow frameworks at the application layer, which is categorically different from hardware-level AI accelerator optimization. The low-latency work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, caching) is the closest analog to performance engineering, but it is web-tier performance, not silicon or compute-architecture performance.
strengths: Strong low-latency software engineering background (async I/O, fiber-based concurrency, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby)
Broad polyglot experience (C/C++, DLang, TypeScript, Java) suggests adaptability across system layers
Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) would support a rigorous engineering culture
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is a core, non-negotiable requirement of the role
No background in deep learning workloads, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD systems — the preferred qualifications are entirely absent
All performance optimization experience is at the application/network layer; no evidence of hardware-software co-design or silicon-level performance analysis

key_factors.factor: No AI/DNN workload or GPU architecture background
